title: Census | Stats NZ
content:
The New Zealand Census of Population and Dwellings is the official count of the people and dwellings in New Zealand. Held every five years, census data helps us understand how, as a country, we are changing over time. The information collected in a census is used by government agencies, local authorities, businesses, iwi, community organisations, and the public to develop and implement new policies, research, planning, and decisions about services in areas like health, education, housing, and transport.
- Census news and publications
- Tools with census data

- More information about the census
Introduction to the New Zealand census explains why we need to hold a census, who's counted, the census cycle, and ongoing development.
New Zealand’s 2023 Census of Population and Dwellings was held on 7 March 2023 (Census Day). The first data was released on 29 May 2024. More data will be released later in 2024, and in 2025.

## Data and information releases
New Zealand's 2018 Census of Population and Dwellings was held on 6 March 2018.

## Census news and publications

## Agricultural snapshot: Insights into farmers’ lives
Farmers across New Zealand will soon participate in the 2026 Agricultural Production Survey. This article uses statistics drawn from several Stats NZ datasets to provide insights into trends in farmers’ employment, wellbeing, income, and other measures.

## Better data for ethnic communities
The data needs of Ethnic Communities are the focus of a new relationship agreement between Stats NZ and the Ministry for Ethnic Communities.

## Homeless and Transient Populations Data Advisory Group: Expression of interest
Stats NZ invites expressions of interest to join a new Homeless and Transient Populations Data Advisory Group.

## Pacific Data Advisory Group: Expression of interest
Stats NZ invites expressions of interest to join a new Pacific Data Advisory Group.

## Rainbow Data Advisory Group: Expression of interest
Stats NZ invites expressions of interest to join a new Rainbow Data Advisory Group.

## Communities are an essential voice in New Zealand’s data system
The creation of Pacific, rainbow, and homeless community advisory groups marks a significant step in Stats NZ’s commitment to a modern, inclusive census.

## Working with communities to design the next census
A newly formed Crown-Māori Statistical Design Forum met last month for its inaugural hui in Wellington. The forum is one of several collaborative design groups being established by Stats NZ to help ensure that the data needs and perspectives of communities are included in the design of the census.

## Creating household and family information from admin data: Progress, limitations, and next census
Provides an update of our current understanding of how well administrative (admin) data can produce household and family statistics, compared with past censuses.

## Information about data
DataInfo+ has metadata about our statistical collections and classifications, and questionnaires and forms.
DataInfo+ concepts gives definitions of terms used by Stats NZ.
Population concepts – DataInfo+ defines the census usually resident population count.

## More information about the census
Security, privacy, and confidentiality of census data explains how we keep the personal information we collect in the census confidential and secure.
Census glossary describes the terms we use when we talk about the census.

## Census enquiries and updates
For more information visit the official census website: www.census.govt.nz .
If you have a question about the 2023 Census, or any previous census, you can email us on: info@stats.govt.nz
Subscribe to our Census Advisory newsletter to receive regular updates about our work.
For media enquiries about the census, please email media@stats.govt.nz or call 021 285 9191 .
